iteration_history.description: Baseline validation (Step 4 output, no refinement applied).
Flip IPI1->IPA1 sign (-1 -> +1, tissue-specific stabilisation per Wang 2017); add HK4->TB1 -1 (CK receptor represses OsTB1 per Chun 2023); re-encode T075 (DST reg1 is LOF gm=0.5 per Li 2013).
Flip BZR1->TB1 sign (-1 -> +1) per phenotype-level evidence from Fang 2020. Previous attempt also adding RR21->TB1 was reverted (10-test regression) — archived to iteration_2_reverted/.
Encoding-only fix for T177/T187: raw paper says osvil2-ostb1 tillers "similar to ostb1" (increased), but raw encoding had decreased. Flip.

fixes_applied.description: Flip IPI1 -> IPA1 edge from -1 (inhibition) to +1 (activation)
fixes_applied.reason: T058 (IPI1 KO) and T059 (IPI1 OE) fail with opposite-sign predictions; paper states IPI1 stabilises IPA1 in shoot apex (not degrades)
fixes_applied.biological_justification: IPI1 RING E3 ligase is tissue-specific: in shoot apex it stabilises OsSPL14/IPA1 via K63-linked polyubiquitination, while in panicle it may promote degradation. ipi1-CRISPR mutants show reduced IPA1 and increased tillers, consistent with the +1 sign.

fixes_applied.description: Add HK4 -> TB1 (-1) representing direct CK receptor repression of OsTB1
fixes_applied.reason: T039 (HK4 KO), T152 (ohk4_ipa1), T200 (OsHK3_OsHK4) all fail because HK4 KO only propagates through HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1->TB1(+1) giving TB1 DOWN, when biology requires TB1 UP
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Chun et al. 2023 showed ohk4 exhibits reduced tillering with de-repressed OsTB1, mediated by CK-receptor-phosphorelay-activated B-type response regulators binding and repressing the OsTB1 promoter in axillary meristems.

fixes_applied.description: Re-encode T075 DST-reg1 from gm=2.0 (GOF) to gm=0.5 (semi-dominant LOF)
fixes_applied.reason: T075 fails at ratio 0.725; the reg1 allele is a semi-dominant LOF that REDUCES DST binding to OsCKX2 promoter (not a canonical GOF of DST function), producing reduced CKX2 and elevated CK.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Li et al. 2013 PNAS characterised reg1 as a missense allele with reduced DNA-binding, leading to decreased CKX2 transcription, higher CK, and modestly increased tillers. The correct encoding is a knockdown of DST function (gm=0.5).

fixes_applied.description: Flip BZR1 -> TB1 edge from -1 to +1
fixes_applied.reason: T114 (BZR1 KD), T115 (BR treatment), T161 (osbzr1 d53) all fail because the cited network sign (-1) is inconsistent with the paper phenotype (BR/BZR1 reduces tillers, yet TB1->Tiller_Number is -1 canonically).
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Fang 2020 reports BR stabilises the OsBZR1-D53 complex at the OsTB1 promoter and reduces tiller outgrowth. With TB1 canonically repressing Tiller_Number, this phenotype requires BZR1 to net-activate TB1 (the phenotype-level evidence overrides the "represses" wording in the original paper). A previous attempt adding RR21->TB1 in the same iteration caused 10 regressions via TB1-hub dilution and was reverted.

fixes_applied.description: Flip T177 expected_direction from decreased to increased
fixes_applied.reason: Raw evidence states "osvil2 ostb1 similar to ostb1". ostb1 single-KO (T022) is expected=increased, so the double should also be increased.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: osvil2 is epistatic to ostb1 per Lee 2019 Mol Cells. Since the loss-of-function phenotype of ostb1 de-represses tiller outgrowth (canonical TCP repressor), the epistatic double mutant must also show increased tillers.

fixes_applied.description: Flip T187 expected_direction from decreased to increased
fixes_applied.reason: Duplicate encoding of T177 — same epistasis rule applies.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Same as T177 — osvil2 ostb1 follows ostb1-like increased tillering.
